original: NASA launches Voyager 1 on a mission to initially fly by Jupiter and Saturn, later including Uranus and Neptune, and in 2012, it becomes the first human-made object to leave the solar system
Eddie Hill sets a propeller-driven boat water speed record of 229 mph (368.54 km/h)
NASA's Space Shuttle Discovery completes its first spaceflight, STS-41-D, with a landing at Edwards Air Force Base, California
Jingyi Le swims a world record in the 100 m women's freestyle in 54.01 seconds
World's longest road tunnel, St. Gotthard in the Swiss Alps, opens
American sailor Charles J. French (22) swims for over six hours in waters near Guadalcanal while towing a life raft with fifteen survivors from his US Navy ship, which is sunk by Japanese gunfire
Oakland A's pitcher Matt Keough beats the Milwaukee Brewers 6-1 for his first win after 14 straight losses, ending 1978 with 18 defeats, one shy of the MLB record of 19
Elmer Trettr sets the record for the highest terminal velocity at 201.34 mph (324.03 km/h)
Graham Salmon, British blind runner (fastest 100m by a blind man), born in London (d. 1999)
Canada puts its first gold bullion coin on sale
The Staffordshire Hoard, the largest Anglo-Saxon gold and silver metalwork hoard ever found with over 4,600 items, is discovered on farmland near Hammerwich, England
"The Huckleberry Hound Show" byHanna-Barberafeaturing Yogi Bear premieres on US TV
Cassius Clay [Muhammad Ali] defeats three-time European champion Zbigniew Pietrzykowski of Poland by unanimous points decision to win the Olympic light heavyweight boxing gold medal at the Rome Games
Jim Henson's "The Muppet Show" premieres on television with Mia Farrow as the guest star
Hurricane Irma becomes one of the most powerful hurricanes ever recorded in the Atlantic Basin region with winds of 185 mph (280 km/h)
Claudette Colvin, American civil rights activist arrested at 15 for refusing to give her seat on a bus to a white woman (nine months before Rosa Parks), born in Montgomery, Alabama[1]

Saint Louis University quarterback Bradbury Robinson throws the first legal forward pass in the history of American football for a touchdown to Jack Schneider at Carroll College in Waukesha, Wisconsin; Saint Louis wins 22-0
Eighth NASA Space Shuttle Mission: Challenger 3 lands at Edwards AFB
SF wide receiverJerry Ricecatches 2 touchdown passes and runs for another score in the 49ers' 44-14 rout of the Raiders, surpassing Jim Brown as the NFL's career TD leader with 127
Barry Callebaut announces a fourth type of chocolate, "Ruby," made from the Ruby cocoa bean
New theory suggests the Loch Ness Monster may be a giant eel after DNA study reveals no plesiosaur or sturgeon DNA found
American pilotWiley Postdiscovers the jet stream while flying at high altitude near Chicago, Illinois
Stuart Freeborn, English motion picture make-up artist (Star Wars: Yoda, Jabba the Hut), born in London, England (d. 2013)
Kim Maher, American softball infielder (Olympic gold 1996), born in Saigon, Vietnam
John Cage's musical composition "As Slow as Possible", intended to be played for 639 years, begins at St. Burchardi Church, Halberstadt, Germany
Lester A. Pelton, American inventor (water wheel for hydroelectricity), born in Vermilion, Ohio (d. 1908)[1]
Steve Denton, American tennis player (world's fastest tennis serve-138 mph), born in Kingsville, Texas
New York Mets' Don Hahn hits his first inside-the-park home run at Phillies Vet
112°F (44°C) in Centerville, Alabama (state record)
38.7 inches (98.3 cm) of rainfall over several days at Yankeetown, Florida (state record)
German archaeologist Carl Mauch is the first European to explore the ruins of the medieval Shona city of Great Zimbabwe, the largest archaeological site in Sub-Saharan Africa
Philadelphia Athletics player Jack Coombs begins a record streak of 53 consecutive shutout innings
First privately operated atomic reactor becomes operational in Raleigh, North Carolina
Estimated 15 cm (6 inches) of rainfall in Bug Point, Utah (state record)
Kuini Ngawai Hono i te Po (27) is named the new Māori Monarch as her father, King Tuheitia, is buried at Tūrangawaewae Marae, New Zealand
Future world middleweight boxing championNino Benvenutiof Italy defeats Yuri Radonyak of the Soviet Union to win the welterweight gold medal at the Rome Olympics
Stewart Copeland's opera "Electric Saint" about the life ofNikola Tesla, with libretto by Jonathan Moore, premieres at the Deutsches Nationaltheater in Weimar, Germany
Firebreaks finally bring the Great Fire of London under control, leaving 13,200 houses destroyed and eight dead
Andrian Nikolayev, Soviet cosmonaut (Vostok III, Soyuz 9), born in Shorshely, Russia (d. 2004)
Dave Dryden, Canadian ice hockey goaltender (designer first mask-cage combination goalie mask; Chicago Black Hawks, Buffalo Sabres, Edmonton Oilers), born in Hamilton, Ontario (d. 2022)
(Louis) "Chip" Davis, American new age musican, composer (Mannheim Steamroller), and country music songwriter (C. W. McCall - "Convoy"), born in Hamler, Ohio
Twelve of the thirteen American colonies adopt a trade embargo against Great Britain at the First Continental Congress in Carpenters' Hall, Philadelphia, Pennsylvania
American Arctic explorer George Washington De Long on board the Jeannette becomes trapped with his crew in pack ice during an attempt to reach the North Pole
Walter Johnsonsets MLB strikeout record at 2,287
Iron ore is discovered in Minnesota's Mesabi Mountains
Jerry Lewis's first Muscular Dystrophy Labor Day telethon raises $1 million
Napoleon "Nap" Lajoie, American Baseball HOF second baseman (Triple Crown 1901; AL batting champion 1901–04, 10; Philadelphia A's, Cleveland Naps), born in Woonsocket, Rhode Island (d. 1959)
Frank Thomas, American animator, born in Fresno, California (d. 2004)
Gail Kubik, American composer (Gerald McBoing Boing), born in South Coffeyville, Oklahoma (d. 1984)
Art Rupe [Goldberg], American gospel, R&B, and early rock record producer and label executive (Specialty Records - Little Richard, Sam Cooke, Lloyd Price), born in Greensburg, Pennsylvania (d. 2022)
Dweezil Zappa, American rock guitarist and son of Frank Zappa, born in Hollywood, California
Liam Lynch, American musician (Sifl and Olly), born in Petersburg, Virginia
Jenny Whittle, Australian basketball center (Olympic bronze 1996), born in Gold Coast, Australia
With the meeting of the Continental Congress, Philadelphia becomes the first capital of the United States
The first public library in the Americas is established when Bishop Palafox y Mendoza donates his personal library of 5,000 volumes to Tridentine colleges in Puebla, Mexico
Southern Blacks, led by Pap Singleton, settle in Kansas
Allies liberate Brussels, Belgium
West Indies lose by one wicket to England in the first one-day cricket international
US Men's Amateur Golf Championship, The Country Club: Jay Sigel wins 8 and 7 over David Tolley
Denmark celebrates the first national Flag Day in memory of the fallen Danes in international operations since 1948
